commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=99d3c765a9425e24530724d2730fd7218ef7b291)
commands.review_summary:
The PR threads iMessage `sendTransport` into the attachment sender, maps `bridge` to the `send-attachment` CLI `dylib` token, and adds argv regression tests.

PR surface: Source +15, Tests +76. Total +91 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main resolves `sendTransport` for RPC text sends but hardcodes `--transport auto` for media `send-attachment`. PR-head source also shows the remaining AppleScript voice/threaded attachment conflict against the upstream CLI contract.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human/contributor action is needed because the remaining blocker includes real macOS proof and upgrade-sensitive iMessage transport behavior that automation cannot prove from the PR branch alone.

target.title: fix(imessage): honor configured send transport for attachment sends
target.body:
## Summary

PR #91783 introduced `channels.imessage.sendTransport` (`auto` / `bridge` / `applescript`) and threaded it into the `imsg rpc send` text path. The symmetric `send-attachment` media path was missed: it still passes a hardcoded `--transport auto`. As a result, an account configured with `sendTransport: "bridge"` or `sendTransport: "applescript"` honors that preference for text sends but silently falls back to `auto` transport selection for every attachment/media send.

This is the same class of omission as #93137 (imessage reply gate applied to text but not the symmetric path): a setting was wired into one delivery path and left out of the adjacent one.

## What changed

- `extensions/imessage/src/send.ts`
  - Add an optional `transport?: IMessageSendTransport` field to `trySendAttachmentForTarget`'s params.
  - Thread the already-resolved `sendTransport` (`account.config.sendTransport ?? "auto"`) from `sendMessageIMessage` into the attachment call, matching the existing RPC text send path.
  - **Map the transport token at the attachment CLI boundary.** OpenClaw config exposes `auto`/`bridge`/`applescript`, but `imsg send-attachment` accepts only `auto`/`dylib`/`applescript` and rejects `bridge`. The two boundaries differ: the JSON-RPC `send` path forwards `bridge` verbatim because the imsg RPC server maps it to the IMCore bridge internally, whereas the CLI expects the `dylib` token (the bridge is implemented by the injected helper dylib). A small `toAttachmentTransport()` adapter maps `bridge → dylib` and passes `auto`/`applescript` through unchanged, then feeds the result to the `send-attachment` argv.
- `extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts`
  - Add regression coverage: an account with `sendTransport: "bridge"` now sends media through `send-attachment` with `--transport dylib` (not the rejected `bridge`).
  - Keep the existing `sendTransport: "applescript"` → `--transport applescript` case.

## Root cause

In `sendMessageIMessage`, `sendTransport` is resolved once from account config and threaded into the RPC text send `params.transport`. The media branch calls `trySendAttachmentForTarget`, which built the `send-attachment` argv with a literal `"--transport", "auto"`. The resolved `sendTransport` was never passed down, so attachment delivery could not honor `bridge`/`applescript`. A naive forward of the raw value would still break `bridge` users, because the OpenClaw RPC token `bridge` is not a valid `send-attachment` CLI token — the CLI uses `dylib` for the bridge. The fix threads the value **and** normalizes it for the CLI boundary.

## Real behavior proof (real runtime, outside tests)

This is a real Node runtime run (`node --import tsx`, **not** Vitest). A throwaway `.mts` script imports the **real exported** `sendMessageIMessage` from `extensions/imessage/src/send.ts`, installs the real plugin-state runtime, and injects a real `runCliJson` stub that **captures the actual `send-attachment` subprocess argv** at the CLI boundary. It runs the media path once per configured transport (`bridge` / `applescript` / `auto`) and prints the real `--transport` token the attachment path emits.

**Exact command**: `TSX_TSCONFIG_PATH=tsconfig.json node --import tsx extensions/imessage/src/_proof_send_transport.mts`

(`TSX_TSCONFIG_PATH` only points the loader at the repo's `tsconfig.json` so `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` resolves to the same `src/*.ts` the test suite uses in a source checkout; it does not touch the send path.)

The script imports the real function and captures the subprocess argv per configured value:

```ts
import { sendMessageIMessage } from "./send.js";
import { installIMessageStateRuntimeForTest } from "./test-support/runtime.js";

installIMessageStateRuntimeForTest();
async function runWith(configured: "bridge" | "applescript" | "auto") {
  const captured: string[][] = [];
  const runCliJson = async (args: readonly string[]) => {
    captured.push([...args]);
    return { messageId: "p:0/media-guid", transferGuid: "transfer-1" };
  };
  await sendMessageIMessage("chat_guid:chat-1", "", {
    config: { channels: { imessage: {
      sendTransport: "auto", accounts: { work: { sendTransport: configured } } } } } as never,
    accountId: "work",
    mediaUrl: "/tmp/image.png",
    resolveAttachmentImpl: async () => ({ path: "/tmp/image.png", contentType: "image/png" }),
    runCliJson,
  });
  // print the captured send-attachment argv and its --transport value
}
```

**Real terminal output — after fix (HEAD `99d3c76`)**:

```
configured sendTransport=bridge
  captured send-attachment argv = ["send-attachment","--chat","chat-1","--file","/tmp/image.png","--transport","dylib"]
  --transport actual value      = dylib
configured sendTransport=applescript
  captured send-attachment argv = ["send-attachment","--chat","chat-1","--file","/tmp/image.png","--transport","applescript"]
  --transport actual value      = applescript
configured sendTransport=auto
  captured send-attachment argv = ["send-attachment","--chat","chat-1","--file","/tmp/image.png","--transport","auto"]
  --transport actual value      = auto

=== summary ===
bridge      -> dylib   (expected dylib: bridge maps to the CLI dylib token)
applescript -> applescript   (expected applescript: passes through unchanged)
auto        -> auto   (expected auto: passes through unchanged)

PASS: attachment boundary maps bridge->dylib and passes auto/applescript through.
```

**Negative control — same script, source temporarily reverted to forward the raw token** (then restored; HEAD unchanged):

```
configured sendTransport=bridge
  captured send-attachment argv = ["send-attachment","--chat","chat-1","--file","/tmp/image.png","--transport","bridge"]
  --transport actual value      = bridge
=== summary ===
bridge      -> bridge   (expected dylib: bridge maps to the CLI dylib token)
FAIL: unexpected transport mapping at the attachment boundary.
```

The captured real subprocess argv shows the mapping is load-bearing: with the fix, a `bridge` account's `send-attachment` invocation carries `--transport dylib` (the CLI-supported bridge token); without it, the same media path emits `--transport bridge`, which upstream `send-attachment` rejects. `applescript` and `auto` pass through unchanged in both runs. The proof script is not committed to the PR.

> Note: This proof drives the real TypeScript send path and the real CLI-argv boundary, but it does not exercise a live macOS device (Messages.app / injected dylib), which is unavailable in this environment. It proves the transport-token contract end to end at the `send-attachment` argv boundary; it does not claim a live on-device media delivery.

## Real behavior proof (Vitest)

The test drives the real exported `sendMessageIMessage` and captures the exact `send-attachment` subprocess argv through the injected `runCliJson` boundary.

**Exact command**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts`

**Result (after fix)**: 45 passed, including the new `maps the configured bridge transport to the send-attachment dylib token` and the retained `honors the configured send transport for send-attachment media` (applescript).

```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  45 passed (45)
```

**Negative control**: with the test kept but the source reverted to forward the raw token, only the new bridge assertion fails (it captures `bridge` instead of `dylib`); restoring the mapping returns the suite to 45 passed.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts` — 45 passed.
- Negative control (revert mapping, keep test) — only the new bridge test fails; restore — 45 passed again.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs` — clean (exit 0).
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/imessage/src/send.ts extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts` — 0 warnings, 0 errors.
- `oxfmt --check extensions/imessage/src/send.ts extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts` — all files correctly formatted.

## Risk checklist

- **Default behavior**: unchanged. `params.transport ?? "auto"` keeps `auto` when `sendTransport` is unset, and `toAttachmentTransport("auto") === "auto"`; all existing attachment tests (which assert `--transport auto`) still pass.
- **Compatibility (addresses the prior review)**: a `bridge` account no longer forwards the rejected `bridge` token to `send-attachment`; it is mapped to the CLI's `dylib` token, so media sends honor the configured bridge path instead of failing at delivery time.
- **Scope**: 2 files, +48 / -1. No public API or config schema change (`sendTransport` already exists from #91783). Only the iMessage `send-attachment` argv changes, behind a narrow adapter at the attachment boundary.
target.author: alix-007

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
